Off Calendar Aug-01-2011 Continued To Aug-22-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-06-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Van Dyk for Rosalinn Moorehead (Personal Representative).

Mr. Van Dyk stated that the personal representative was in Idaho and couldn't appear. The Court stated that it had reviewed the petition and the request for reimbursement. Discussions regarding the back taxes were held. The Court stated that what appeared to be missing were the taxes from 1988 through 1993. The Court approved the $9,680 in property taxes, found that the $1,500 travel request was reasonable, allowed $958 for PG & E, but did not allow the additional $500. Discussions were held regarding the request for $6000 in costs for the underground wiring. The Court stated that it would not allow the $6000 because there was no documentation to substantiate the request, but noted that it could be revisited at a later time subject to proof. Mr. Van Dyk stated that there was an error on the proposed order and that the reserve for the taxes should be $60,000. He stated that the accountant had informed him the taxes would be less than $60,000 but he did not know the exact amount. The Court approved the $60,000 reserve. The Court granted the petition (subject to review of the proposed order presented today in open court).

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy S. Neu.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-10-2009 Continued To Mar-03-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (197 Laidley St., San Francisco, Ca 94131)
Off Calendar Jun-26-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:Paul Vandyk for Petitioner.

Mr. Vandyk stated that the buyers were present and waived formal notice. The sale was confirmed at $726,000.00. An additional bond in the amount of $265,000.00 is required, (per Examiners notes). The petition is granted subject to a written order and filing of the bond.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/ S. Ravinantapricha. Bailiff: J. Garrido.
